actions.help: display information messages.
do not display information messages or progress status. Alternatively, this can be achieved by setting the MRTRIX_QUIET environment variable to a non-empty string.
display debugging messages.
force overwrite of output files.
use this number of threads in multi-threaded applications (set to 0 to disable multi-threading).
temporarily set the value of an MRtrix config file entry.
display this information page and exit.
display version information and exit.
do not delete intermediate files during script execution, and do not delete scratch directory at script completion.
manually specify the path in which to generate the scratch directory.
continue the script from a previous execution; must provide the scratch directory path, and the name of the last successfully-generated file.
Provide an initial mask for response voxel selection
Output an image showing the final voxel selection(s)
The b-value(s) to use in response function estimation (comma-separated list in case of multiple b-values, b=0 must be included explicitly)
The maximum harmonic degree(s) for response function estimation (comma-separated list in case of multiple b-values)
Provide the diffusion gradient table in MRtrix format
Provide the diffusion gradient table in FSL bvecs/bvals format

Select the algorithm to be used to complete the script operation; additional details and options become available once an algorithm is nominated. Options are: dhollander, fa, manual, msmt_5tt, tax, tournier

Provide an initial mask for response voxel selection
Output an image showing the final voxel selection(s)

The maximum harmonic degree(s) for response function estimation (comma-separated list in case of multiple b-values)
Provide the diffusion gradient table in MRtrix format
Provide the diffusion gradient table in FSL bvecs/bvals format
Input DWI dataset
Output single-fibre WM response function text file
Output GM response function text file
Output CSF response function text file
Number of erosion passes to apply to initial (whole brain) mask. Set to 0 to not erode the brain mask. (default: 3)
FA threshold for crude WM versus GM-CSF separation. (default: 0.2)
Final number of single-fibre WM voxels to select, as a percentage of refined WM. (default: 0.5 per cent)
Final number of GM voxels to select, as a percentage of refined GM. (default: 2 per cent)
Final number of CSF voxels to select, as a percentage of refined CSF. (default: 10 per cent)
Use external dwi2response algorithm for WM single-fibre voxel selection (options: fa, tax, tournier) (default: built-in Dhollander 2019)

mrtrix.synopsis: Estimate response function(s) for spherical deconvolution
description: dwi2response offers different algorithms for performing various types of response function estimation. The name of the algorithm must appear as the first argument on the command-line after 'dwi2response'. The subsequent arguments and options depend on the particular algorithm being invoked.
Each algorithm available has its own help page, including necessary references; e.g. to see the help page of the 'fa' algorithm, type 'dwi2response fa'.
mrtrix.author: Robert E. Smith (robert.smith@florey.edu.au) and Thijs Dhollander (thijs.dhollander@gmail.com)

dhollander.synopsis: Unsupervised estimation of WM, GM and CSF response functions that does not require a T1 image (or segmentation thereof)
description: This is an improved version of the Dhollander et al. (2016) algorithm for unsupervised estimation of WM, GM and CSF response functions, which includes the Dhollander et al. (2019) improvements for single-fibre WM response function estimation (prior to this update, the "dwi2response tournier" algorithm had been utilised specifically for the single-fibre WM response function estimation step).
dhollander.author: Thijs Dhollander (thijs.dhollander@gmail.com)
citations: Dhollander, T.; Raffelt, D. & Connelly, A. Unsupervised 3-tissue response function estimation from single-shell or multi-shell diffusion MR data without a co-registered T1 image. ISMRM Workshop on Breaking the Barriers of Diffusion MRI, 2016, 5
Dhollander, T.; Mito, R.; Raffelt, D. & Connelly, A. Improved white matter response function estimation for 3-tissue constrained spherical deconvolution. Proc Intl Soc Mag Reson Med, 2019, 555

citations: Tournier, J.-D.; Calamante, F.; Gadian, D. G. & Connelly, A. Direct estimation of the fiber orientation density function from diffusion-weighted MRI data using spherical deconvolution. NeuroImage, 2004, 23, 1176-1185

msmt_5tt.synopsis: Derive MSMT-CSD tissue response functions based on a co-registered five-tissue-type (5TT) image

description:
Estimate response function(s) for spherical deconvolution.

dwi2response offers different algorithms for performing various types of response function estimation. The name of the algorithm must appear as the first argument on the command-line after 'dwi2response'. The subsequent arguments and options depend on the particular algorithm being invoked.

Each algorithm available has its own help page, including necessary references; e.g. to see the help page of the 'fa' algorithm, type 'dwi2response fa'.
